title: Forward Model from a System of Ordinary Differential Equations — ode_forward_model
text: derivs Function function(t, y, theta) -> dydt. ODE right hand side; returns numeric vector length state y. theta named parameter vector one realisation. y0 Numeric vector, function(theta) -> numeric. initial state times[1]. function form lets initial condition depend calibrated parameters. times Numeric vector strictly increasing observation times, length least two. first entry initial time; trajectory recorded every entry. param_names Character vector parameter columns model consumes theta, order. Empty (default) disables column check passes theta rows verbatim. observe Function function(traj, theta) -> obs. Maps length(times) x n_state trajectory matrix length-n_obs observation vector. Default reads state variable one every time first. solver Character. "rk4" (default, self-contained) "desolve" (delegates deSolve::ode()). n_steps Integer. Fixed RK4 sub-steps successive observation times (default 10L). Ignored solver = "desolve". Larger values trade speed integration accuracy. desolve_method Character. deSolve::ode() method solver = "desolve" (default "lsoda"). Ignored "rk4". n_obs Integer NA. Known observation dimensionality. NA (default) infers first successful realisation. ... policy arguments forwarded pesto_forward_model() (example on_failure, parallel, n_cores, fidelity, label).

title: Forward Model from a System of Ordinary Differential Equations — ode_forward_model

title: Forward Model from a System of Ordinary Differential Equations — ode_forward_model
text: Wraps user-supplied ODE right-hand side typed pesto_forward_model() whose forward map integrates system fixed time grid reads observation vector resulting state trajectory. generic differential_equations template crop_growth_forward_model() seir_forward_model() specialise; use directly compartmental mechanistic ODE. derivative function derivs signature function(t, y, theta) -> dydt, returning numeric vector length state y. initial state y0 may plain numeric vector (shared across realisations) function function(theta) -> y0 starting condition calibrated (example unknown initial inoculum). observation map observe signature function(traj, theta) -> obs, traj length(times) x length(y0) matrix states integration time (column order matching y0); default reads first state variable every time first. Two integrators available. default solver = "rk4" self-contained classical fourth-order Runge-Kutta n_steps fixed sub-steps successive observation times – external dependency, deterministic, adequate smooth non-stiff systems templates target. solver = "desolve" delegates deSolve::ode() (optional Suggests dependency) method desolve_method, brings adaptive step control stiff solvers ("lsoda", default, switches non-stiff stiff automatically). realisation whose integration fails returns non-finite trajectory reported NA row, pesto_evaluate() pesto_ies_callback() handle failure policy.

code: # Exponential decay dy/dt = -k y, observed at five times. Calibrate k. fm <- ode_forward_model(   derivs      = function(t, y, theta) -theta[["k"]] * y,   y0          = c(y = 1),   times       = seq(0, 4, by = 1),   param_names = "k" ) theta <- matrix(c(0.5, 1.0), ncol = 1L, dimnames = list(NULL, "k")) pesto_evaluate(fm, theta) #>           [,1]      [,2]      [,3]       [,4] #> [1,] 0.6065307 0.3678795 0.2231302 0.13533530 #> [2,] 0.3678798 0.1353355 0.0497872 0.01831571 #> attr(,"n_failures") #> [1] 0 #> attr(,"fail_idx") #> integer(0)

title: PESTO Ensemble Manifest (S7 class) — pesto_ensemble_manifest
text: schema_version Character. Semantic version manifest schema (default "1.1.0"; 1.1.0 adds optional obs_schema grounding descriptor 1.0.0). run_id Character. Short identifier (auto-generated as_manifest() supplied). obs_schema Optional grounded semantic descriptor (NULL). list(outputs = <data.frame>, params = <data.frame>) giving meaning output / parameter column downstream consumers can check correspondence (unit + quantity) name rather trusting positional convention. outputs columns: name, quantity, unit (+ optional provenance verified_on, oracle_kind, evidence_path); params columns: name, apsim_node, unit (+ optional provenance). Build one pesto_obs_schema(). descriptor provenance metadata — folded data_hash (correspondence grounded second manifest agrees units, self-hash). Default NULL (absent). params data.frame / data.table. Parameter ensemble; first column real_name, subsequent columns one per parameter. outputs data.frame / data.table. Simulated observation ensemble (shape conventions params). weights Named numeric. IES observation weights (1/obs_sd). obs_target Named numeric. Target observations. seed Integer scalar. RNG seed used (NA_integer_ unrecorded). data_hash Character. sha256:... checksum (params, outputs, weights, obs_target, seed). fidelity Structured provenance list NULL. multi-fidelity run pesto_ies_callback() records list(type, schedule, final_level, n_levels, costs); NULL single-fidelity run. legacy named-numeric tag still accepted. apsim_version Character. APSIM version string (NA_character_ forward model non-APSIM). pesto_version Character. PESTO package version produced run. timestamp POSIXct. Construction time. method Character. One "ies_callback", "ies_pst", "mda", "surrogate". noptmax Integer. Number IES iterations. lambda_schedule Numeric vector. Marquardt lambdas per iteration. failure_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction forward evaluations returned NA.

title: PESTO Ensemble Manifest (S7 class) — pesto_ensemble_manifest
text: See write_manifest() / read_manifest() YAML serialisation verify_manifest() integrity checking. YAML file authoritative human-readable entry; params, outputs, assimilation context (weights + obs_target) emitted sidecar files whose relative paths recorded inside YAML. Three sidecar modes: format = "rds" (default, verifiable) — RDS . IEEE 754 doubles round-trip bit-exactly; SHA-256 integrity check holds. format = "" (verifiable) — RDS sidecars plus parallel *_inspection.csv files. Hash bound RDS; CSVs decorative. format = "csv_unverified" (verifiable, renamed "csv" PESTO 0.3.2) — CSV-sidecars. Hash recorded recomputed disk (CSV formatter precision loss ~1 ULP). verify_manifest() returns ok = NA + message. Use one-way export non-R analysts; round-trip integrity construction broken. YAML carries explicit integrity: field ("verifiable" / "not_verifiable") derived format downstream non-R tools can branch integrity contract without needing know PESTO-specific format vocabulary.

title: PESTO Ensemble Manifest (S7 class) — pesto_ensemble_manifest
code: set.seed(1) npar <- 2L; nobs <- 3L; nreal <- 30L G <- matrix(stats::rnorm(nobs * npar), nobs, npar) y <- as.numeric(G %*% c(0.5, -1.0)) + stats::rnorm(nobs, sd = 0.05) names(y) <- paste0("o", seq_len(nobs)) prior <- matrix(stats::rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar,                 dimnames = list(NULL, c("a", "b"))) fit <- pesto_ies_callback(function(t) t %*% t(G), prior, y, 0.05,                           noptmax = 3, verbose = FALSE) m <- as_manifest(fit, seed = 1L) verify_manifest(m)$ok #> [1] TRUE

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: Drives Iterative Ensemble Smoother entirely R, using user-supplied forward model callable instead PEST++ .pst-file invocation cycle. iteration:

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
code: pesto_ies_callback(   forward_model,   prior_ensemble,   obs,   obs_sd,   noptmax = 4L,   lambda = 1,   fidelity_schedule = NULL,   parcov = NULL,   eigthresh = 1e-06,   use_approx = TRUE,   inflation = NULL,   localisation = NULL,   on_failure = c("na", "stop"),   verbose = TRUE,   phi_tol = NULL )

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: forward_model One : function signature function(theta) -> obs (theta nreal x npar numeric matrix obs nreal x nobs numeric matrix); pesto_forward_model() (typed contract object, e.g. one carrying parallel = "multicore" evaluation strategy); pesto_multifidelity_model() (see fidelity_schedule). bare function auto-wrapped via as_forward_model() call's on_failure. Failed realisations may return rows NA; driver tolerates (see on_failure). run realisations parallel, pass pesto_forward_model built parallel = "multicore" rather bare function. prior_ensemble Matrix data.table, nreal x npar. Columns parameters; optional real_name column preserved present. Column names supply parameter names. warm-start, pass parameter columns previous run's par_ensemble prior next: posterior ensemble valid prior ensemble. obs Named numeric vector. Target observations. obs_sd Numeric scalar vector length nobs. Observation standard deviation(s); IES weights 1/obs_sd. noptmax Integer. Number IES iterations (default 4). lambda Numeric scalar vector. Marquardt lambda per iteration. scalar recycled; vector shorter noptmax right-padded last value (default 1.0). fidelity_schedule Integer vector NULL. consulted forward_model pesto_multifidelity_model(): fidelity level evaluate iteration (recycled / right-padded noptmax). NULL (default) uses highest fidelity every iteration. Ignored otherwise. parcov Numeric vector length npar, diagonal prior parameter covariance. Defaults column-wise variance prior_ensemble; zero negative entries replaced 1.0. eigthresh Numeric. SVD eigenvalue truncation threshold (default 1e-6). use_approx Logical. TRUE (default), skip prior-scaling correction (upgrade_2); matches typical pestpp-ies default. inflation pesto_inflation() specification, NULL (default) covariance inflation. Inflation counteracts ensemble -dispersion – progressive collapse posterior spread finite-ensemble smoother suffers. NULL leaves update identical un-inflated smoother. localisation pesto_localisation() specification, NULL (default) localisation. Localisation tapers Kalman gain suppress spurious finite-sample parameter-observation correlations; active path uses ensemble_solution_localised() (approximate / upgrade_1 form), non-NULL localisation use_approx = FALSE warns drops null-space correction. on_failure Character. "na" (default) carries failed realisations forward unchanged proceeds; "stop" aborts failure. verbose Logical. Print per-iteration phi summaries. phi_tol Numeric scalar NULL. Optional convergence tolerance: non-NULL, iteration stops early relative reduction mean objective function (phi) successive iterations falls phi_tol – phi-reduction stopping rule White (2018). NULL (default) runs full noptmax iterations, leaving update byte-identical unchecked smoother. Use smaller phi_tol demand iterations, larger one stop sooner.

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: list class c("pesto_ies_callback_result", "pesto_ies_result") components: phi data.table per-realisation phi iteration. par_ensemble Final parameter ensemble (data.table). obs_ensemble Final simulated-observation ensemble (data.table). iterations List per-iteration metadata: lambda, mean_phi, n_failures, dispersion diagnostics spread_ess / spread_ess_ratio (ensemble_spread_ess()), plus inflation_method / inflation_factor / retention localisation / loc_threshold / loc_frac_active countermeasures active. runtime_seconds Total wall-clock runtime. n_forward_evals Total number realisation-level forward evaluations across iterations (including final refresh). failure_rate Fraction forward evaluations returned NA. converged Logical: TRUE run stopped early phi_tol convergence criterion; FALSE phi_tol NULL full noptmax reached. n_iterations Number IES iterations actually run (fewer noptmax convergence checker stopped run early). fidelity pesto_multifidelity_model() run, provenance list list(type, schedule, final_level, n_levels, costs) recording realised per-iteration fidelity schedule; NULL single-fidelity run. Consumed as_manifest() populate manifest contract.

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: Evaluates forward_model(par_ensemble) obtain simulated observations. Forms parameter / observation anomalies residuals. Calls C++ kernel ensemble_solution() (GLM form, Chen & Oliver 2013) compute nreal x npar upgrade. Adds upgrade current ensemble. classic .pst-file path remains available via pesto_ies() full PEST++ compatibility. Use callback driver forward model R function (e.g. apsimx wrapper apsim_callback(), Python bridge, synthetic test problem) per-realisation file-/O overhead .pst path bottleneck. Phase-1 behaviour: single lambda per iteration (user-supplied schedule). line-search ies_lambda_mults matching pestpp-ies planned Phase-2 enhancement; common case well-behaved forward model lambda = 1, GLM update reduces phi reliably (see vignette apsim-callback).

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: forward_model pesto_multifidelity_model(), fidelity_schedule selects fidelity level iteration evaluates – recycled / padded integer vector, one entry per iteration (default: highest fidelity every iteration, .e. exactly single-fidelity behaviour). supports fidelity ramping (cheap early iterations, expensive late ones); final ensemble refresh always uses highest fidelity returned posterior full resolution. control-variate combiner mf_control_variate() plug-point bias-corrected surrogate cascades.

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: Chen, Y. & Oliver, D.S. (2013). Levenberg-Marquardt forms iterative ensemble smoother efficient history matching uncertainty quantification. Computational Geosciences, 17(4), 689–703. White, J.T. (2018). model-independent iterative ensemble smoother efficient history-matching uncertainty quantification high dimensions. Environmental Modelling & Software, 109, 191–201.

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
code: # Linear-Gaussian recovery toy set.seed(1) npar <- 3; nobs <- 6; nreal <- 80 G <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * npar), nobs, npar) theta_true <- c(1.0, -0.5, 2.0) y <- as.numeric(G %*% theta_true) + rnorm(nobs, sd = 0.05) f <- function(theta) theta %*% t(G) prior <- matrix(rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar,                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("p", 1:npar))) fit <- pesto_ies_callback(   forward_model = f, prior_ensemble = prior,   obs = setNames(y, paste0("o", 1:nobs)), obs_sd = 0.05,   noptmax = 5, verbose = FALSE ) colMeans(as.matrix(fit$par_ensemble[, -1]))  # should approach theta_true #>         p1         p2         p3  #>  1.0136905 -0.4936753  1.9847267

text: Assimilates observations time-ordered windows static parameter ensemble. window runs IES Gauss-Levenberg-Marquardt update using window's observation block; updated ensemble carried forward prior next window. filtering analogue pesto_ies_callback(): instead one batch assimilation observations, posterior refined window window available .

title: Run a Sequential (Filter-Mode) Iterative Ensemble Smoother — pesto_ies_filter
text: forward_model function function(theta) -> obs, pesto_forward_model(), pesto_multifidelity_model() (see fidelity_schedule). model maps full nreal x npar parameter matrix full nreal x nobs observation matrix; windows select columns output. bare function auto-wrapped via as_forward_model(). See pesto_ies_callback() contract. prior_ensemble Matrix data.table, nreal x npar. obs Named numeric vector length nobs. full set target observations; windows indexes . obs_sd Numeric scalar length-nobs vector. Observation standard deviation(s); weights 1 / obs_sd. windows list integer vectors. element gives observation indices (obs, 1-based) assimilated window, assimilation order. Windows must disjoint (observation assimilated twice) need cover obs. window_noptmax Integer scalar vector. IES iterations within window (default 1L, pure filter; > 1 gives iterated filter per window). scalar recycled; short vector right-padded last value. lambda Numeric scalar per-window vector. Marquardt lambda (default 1.0; recycled / right-padded across windows). fidelity_schedule Integer vector NULL. Per-window fidelity level pesto_multifidelity_model() (see Multi-fidelity). parcov Numeric vector length npar, diagonal prior parameter covariance. Defaults prior ensemble's column-wise variance (non-positive entries replaced 1). eigthresh Numeric. SVD eigenvalue truncation (default 1e-6). use_approx Logical. TRUE (default) skip prior-scaling correction, matching pestpp-ies default. inflation pesto_inflation() specification, NULL (default). Applied within window's inner update counteract ensemble -dispersion. See pesto_ies_callback(). localisation pesto_localisation() specification, NULL (default). Tapers per-window Kalman gain suppress spurious finite-sample correlations. See pesto_ies_callback(). on_failure Character. "na" (default) tolerates failed realisations; "stop" aborts failure. verbose Logical. Print per-window phi summary.

title: Run a Sequential (Filter-Mode) Iterative Ensemble Smoother — pesto_ies_filter
code: # Linear-Gaussian recovery, assimilated in three observation windows. set.seed(1) npar <- 3; nobs <- 9; nreal <- 80 G <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * npar), nobs, npar) theta_true <- c(1.0, -0.5, 2.0) y <- as.numeric(G %*% theta_true) + rnorm(nobs, sd = 0.05) f <- function(theta) theta %*% t(G) prior <- matrix(rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar,                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("p", 1:npar))) fit <- pesto_ies_filter(   forward_model  = f, prior_ensemble = prior,   obs = setNames(y, paste0("o", 1:nobs)), obs_sd = 0.05,   windows = list(1:3, 4:6, 7:9), verbose = FALSE ) # Posterior sd should shrink window over window: vapply(fit$windows, function(w) mean(w$par_sd), numeric(1)) #> [1] 0.052603202 0.003631259 0.003615228

what: Covariance Inflation Specification for IES — pesto_inflation
title: Covariance Inflation Specification for IES — pesto_inflation
text: Builds control object tells pesto_ies_callback() pesto_ies_filter() counteract ensemble -dispersion — progressive collapse posterior spread iterative ensemble smoother suffers finite ensemble. Inflation re-expands post-update parameter spread iteration; default method = "none" leaves update byte-identical un-inflated smoother.

title: Covariance Inflation Specification for IES — pesto_inflation
code: pesto_inflation(   method = c("none", "rtps", "adaptive", "multiplicative"),   alpha = 0.5,   factor = 1,   retention_floor = 0.5,   max_factor = 5 )

title: Covariance Inflation Specification for IES — pesto_inflation
text: method Character. One "none" (default), "rtps", "adaptive", "multiplicative". alpha Numeric [0, 1]. RTPS relaxation coefficient (default 0.5); used method = "rtps". factor Numeric \(\ge\) 1. Fixed inflation factor method = "multiplicative" (default 1, .e. inflation). retention_floor Numeric (0, 1]. Target floor mean spread-retention ratio method = "adaptive" (default 0.5). max_factor Numeric \(\ge\) 1. Upper bound single-iteration inflation factor "rtps" "adaptive" (default 5).

title: Covariance Inflation Specification for IES — pesto_inflation
text: object class "pesto_inflation".

title: Covariance Inflation Specification for IES — pesto_inflation
text: Four methods offered. "rtps" relaxation--prior-spread (Whitaker & Hamill 2012): parameter's posterior anomalies rescaled \(\alpha(\sigma^{b} - \sigma^{})/\sigma^{} + 1\), \(\sigma^{b}\) \(\sigma^{}\) background (pre-update) analysis (post-update) standard deviations. per-parameter, re-inflates directions collapsed hardest, spectrally-aware workhorse. "adaptive" global, magnitude-targeting scheme: measures mean spread-retention ratio \(q = \mathrm{mean}_j(\sigma^{}_j/\sigma^{b}_j)\) , q falls retention_floor, applies single multiplicative factor \(\min(\texttt{max\_factor}, \texttt{retention\_floor}/q)\) restore lost variance magnitude. "multiplicative" applies fixed factor every iteration. "none" disables inflation. companion diagnostic spectral spread-ESS (ensemble_spread_ess()), recorded iteration regardless method: reports effective number variance-carrying directions detects directional collapse. participation ratio invariant global rescaling, global ("multiplicative" / "adaptive") inflation restores variance magnitude spectral shape; "rtps" method reshapes spectrum. two compose well.

title: Covariance Inflation Specification for IES — pesto_inflation
text: Whitaker, J.S. & Hamill, T.M. (2012). Evaluating methods account system errors ensemble data assimilation. Monthly Weather Review, 140(9), 3078–3089.

title: Covariance Localisation Specification for IES — pesto_localisation

"distance" classical distance-based localisation: Gaspari-Cohn taper (gaspari_cohn()) parameter--observation distance matrix, problems metric exists — supply either distances directly par_coords + obs_coords (Euclidean distances computed), together radius.

title: Multi-Fidelity Forward Model (S7 class) — pesto_multifidelity_model
code: cheap     <- function(theta) theta %*% c(1, 1)        # fast, biased expensive <- function(theta) theta %*% c(1, 1) + 0.5  # slow, truth mf <- pesto_multifidelity_model(   levels = list(     pesto_forward_model(fn = cheap,     n_obs = 1L, fidelity = 0L),     pesto_forward_model(fn = expensive, n_obs = 1L, fidelity = 1L)   ),   costs = c(1, 25) ) theta <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 1), nrow = 2L, byrow = TRUE) pesto_evaluate(mf, theta, level = 0L)  # cheap #>      [,1] #> [1,]    1 #> [2,]    1 #> attr(,"n_failures") #> [1] 0 #> attr(,"fail_idx") #> integer(0) pesto_evaluate(mf, theta, level = 1L)  # expensive #>      [,1] #> [1,]  1.5 #> [2,]  1.5 #> attr(,"n_failures") #> [1] 0 #> attr(,"fail_idx") #> integer(0)

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: par_ensemble Numeric matrix (n_par x n_real). Current parameter ensemble (column one realisation). obs_ensemble Numeric matrix (n_obs x n_real). Simulated observations current ensemble. obs_target Numeric vector (n_obs). Target (measured) observation values. weights Numeric vector (n_obs). Observation weights (typically 1 / sd(obs_noise)). lambda Numeric scalar. Marquardt damping parameter. 1.0 textbook GLM update; larger values dampen aggressively.

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: numeric matrix (n_par x n_real): parameter upgrade (add par_ensemble get next iterate).

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: Independent numerical validation. PESTO's C++ kernel can cross-checked reference machine precision (package's regression test tests/testthat/test-ensemble-solution-sign.R paired-seed protocol inst/scripts/i2_paired_seed_check.R rely ). Self-contained pestpp-ies comparison. Vignettes can compare PESTO native IES textbook reference without requiring upstream pestpp-ies binary, making comparison story reproducible CRAN check farms binary-free environments. implementation deliberately pedagogical — readability speed. production work use ensemble_solution() (C++ kernel; \(\sim 35\times\) faster production-scale ensembles).

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: reference uses textbook sign obs_resid = obs - sim positive leading sign upgrade. PESTO's C++ kernel uses equivalent obs_resid = sim - obs leading negative sign (see ?ensemble_solution). yield identical upgrades; difference purely cosmetic. two cross-validated machine precision inst/scripts/i2_paired_seed_check.R.

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: Chen, Y. & Oliver, D. S. (2013). Levenberg-Marquardt forms iterative ensemble smoother efficient history matching uncertainty quantification. Computational Geosciences, 17(4), 689–703. doi:10.1007/s10596-013-9351-5 Evensen, G. (2018). Analysis iterative ensemble smoothers solving inverse problems. Computational Geosciences, 22(3), 885–908. doi:10.1007/s10596-018-9731-y

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
code: # Simple linear inverse problem: identify the true theta given noisy obs. set.seed(20260425L) n_par <- 4L; n_obs <- 12L; n_real <- 30L G <- matrix(rnorm(n_obs * n_par) / sqrt(n_par), n_obs, n_par) theta_true <- rnorm(n_par) y_obs <- as.numeric(G %*% theta_true) + rnorm(n_obs, sd = 0.05) weights <- rep(1 / 0.05, n_obs)  par_ens <- matrix(rnorm(n_par * n_real, sd = 1), n_par, n_real) obs_ens <- G %*% par_ens  # One textbook IES upgrade. upg <- pesto_reference_ies(   par_ensemble = par_ens,   obs_ensemble = obs_ens,   obs_target   = y_obs,   weights      = weights,   lambda       = 1.0 ) dim(upg) #> [1]  4 30  # Apply the upgrade and check phi reduces. par_next <- par_ens + upg obs_next <- G %*% par_next Y <- matrix(rep(y_obs, n_real), n_obs, n_real) phi0 <- mean(compute_phi(Y - obs_ens,  weights)) phi1 <- mean(compute_phi(Y - obs_next, weights)) phi1 < phi0 #> [1] TRUE

title: SEIR Compartmental Forward Model — seir_forward_model
text: ready--use ode_forward_model() specialisation classic Susceptible-Exposed-Infectious-Recovered epidemic model closed population (Anderson & May 1991). four states evolve $$\dot S = -\beta S / N,\quad       \dot E = \beta S / N - \sigma E,$$ $$\dot = \sigma E - \gamma ,\quad \dot R = \gamma ,$$ transmission rate \(\beta\), latency rate \(\sigma\) (mean incubation \(1/\sigma\)), recovery rate \(\gamma\) (mean infectious period \(1/\gamma\)). basic reproduction number \(R_0 = \beta / \gamma\).

title: SEIR Compartmental Forward Model — seir_forward_model

Fixed RK4 sub-steps observation times (default 10L). ... policy arguments forwarded pesto_forward_model() via ode_forward_model().

Supply custom observe ode_forward_model() read different compartment (example incidence \(\sigma E\)).

title: PESTO (development version)
text: Name title grounded lineage. PESTO expands Parameter ESTimation Optimised – PEST approach (PEST = Parameter ESTimation, Doherty 2015) brought R optimised – rather earlier letter--letter backronym. package title now Parameter Estimation Optimised, APSIM Coupling, website tagline, citation, GitHub description match. “Optimised” claim now validated docs. new section PEST PEST++ Comparison vignette (“‘Optimised’ PESTO, quantified”) states precisely optimised, backed measured number maths: wall-clock speed-(reference time / PESTO time) matched accuracy, per-solve cost model behind ; surrogate evaluation-economy; multi-fidelity control-variate variance reduction; convergence-based early stopping. also states limits plainly (fewer solves classic PEST; raw intervals -cover inflation). README’s surrogate feature corrected flat “50-90%” measured, regime-dependent saving. New exported helper pestpp_available() – non-erroring probe PEST++ family executable (e.g. pestpp-ies, pestpp-glm). documented way examples, vignettes, conditional tests skip gracefully external binary installed; every PESTO algorithm runs natively R without one. comparison vignette reframed PEST PEST++ Comparison Simulation Study gains head--head cross-tool benchmark (PESTO vs classic PEST 18.25 vs pestpp-ies 5.2.16) well-posed linear problem non-linear ODE. figures frozen real outputs fixed-seed reproducibility harness, shipped inst/extdata/pestpp_cache/: accuracy parity linear problem, ensemble methods’ advantage linearised GLM non-linear one, two--three-orders--magnitude wall-clock advantage, honest calibration caveat (raw ensemble intervals -cover; apply inflation). new Lineage scope section grounds PESTO PEST (Doherty 2015) PEST++ (White et al. 2020) states algorithm boundary makes comparison fair. Corrected benchmarked pestpp-ies version string vignette 5.2.16 (mislabelled 5.2.25). APSIM positioned PESTO’s flagship simulator partner across public surface, without narrowing model-independent identity. README, Getting started vignette, package DESCRIPTION now name APSIM (coupled -process via apsim_callback() apsimx package) primary worked partnership, alongside hydrological models, crop models, ODE systems. APSIM vignette retitled Calibrating APSIM PESTO previously missing README documentation list (now fixed). code API change. New publication-grade APSIM case study. synthetic-truth recovery experiment (OSSE) calibrating real APSIM Wheat parameters IES: recovers two strong, mechanistically distinct parameters (soil runoff fertiliser nitrogen) truth inside 90% credible interval, correctly flags third (sowing depth) weakly identifiable, demonstrates --sample -coverage correction covariance inflation, emits pesto_ensemble_manifest. Shipped Calibrating APSIM Wheat PESTO vignette (frozen real outputs, builds without APSIM) plus path-free reproducible driver system.file("case_studies/apsim_wheat_calibration.R", package = "PESTO"). second part calibrates physiological parameters (RUE, phenology) real observed biomass apsimx::obsWheat: PESTO’s posterior brackets independent apsimx::optim_apsimx() optimum parameters predicts held-dates, adding uncertainty point optimiser lacks (driver apsim_wheat_realdata.R).

title: PESTO 0.5.0
text: pesto_ies_callback() gains fidelity_schedule (consulted pesto_multifidelity_model): fidelity level evaluated iteration, supporting cheap-early / expensive-late ramping. final ensemble refresh always uses highest fidelity. Fidelity provenance now closes manifest (C2) lineage: multi-fidelity pesto_ies_callback() run records realised schedule result ($fidelity = list(type, schedule, final_level, n_levels, costs)), as_manifest() inherits pesto_ensemble_manifest fidelity slot unless overridden, write_manifest() / read_manifest() round-trip structured record faithfully (outside integrity hash, affect verify_manifest()). Single-fidelity runs record NULL, manifests unchanged. manifest fidelity slot now documented structured provenance list (legacy named-numeric tags still accepted read). Parallel, fault-tolerant ensemble evaluation: pesto_forward_model parallel = "multicore" dispatches realisations across forked workers via parallel::mclapply() L’Ecuyer streams (reproducible RNGkind("L'Ecuyer-CMRG")); serial bulk evaluation unchanged remains default. apsim_callback() now writes realisation unique per-run file, making closure safe drive parallel (wrap pesto_forward_model(parallel = "multicore")). internal bulk--per-row evaluation helper (.eval_forward_safe) retired favour shared engine behind pesto_evaluate(); -error abort message changed `forward_model` failed forward model failed.

text: write_manifest() gains format = c("rds", "", "csv") argument. "rds" (default) preserves current bit-exact binary behaviour. "" writes RDS sidecars plus parallel CSV inspection files (*_inspection.csv); SHA-256 hash stays bound RDS. "csv" writes CSV-sidecars inspection / interchange workflows bit-exact integrity required. what: Ensemble Manifest as a Portable, Versioned Run Record
title: PESTO 0.3.0
text: New S7 class pesto_ensemble_manifest — versioned, hashed, provenance-tracked container ensemble-run output. Slots cover params, outputs, weights, obs_target, seed, data_hash (SHA-256), fidelity, apsim_version, pesto_version, timestamp, plus method context (method, noptmax, lambda_schedule, failure_rate). documented, versioned format downstream tool can read. New as_manifest() — S7 generic method pesto_ies_callback_result. Non-destructive: wraps without mutating source result. New write_manifest() / read_manifest() — YAML+RDS serialisation. YAML carries metadata + relative paths three sidecar RDS files (*_params.rds, *_outputs.rds, *_assim.rds); RDS used preference CSV IEEE 754 doubles round-trip bit-exactly (SHA-256 integrity check otherwise trip CSV formatter precision loss). New verify_manifest() — recomputes SHA-256 (params, outputs, weights, obs_target, seed) compares stored value, returning diagnostic list. Detects post-write tampering sidecar CSVs. New vignette: ensemble-manifest — end--end demo construct → write → read → verify, plus tamper-detection.

Tolerates per-realisation failures via on_failure = c("na", "stop") reports failure_rate result object. Phase-1 behaviour uses single lambda per iteration (user-supplied schedule); full pestpp-ies-style lambda line-search planned Phase-2 enhancement. Per-realisation template copy, parameter edit via apsimx::edit_apsimx() / edit_apsim(), run, extraction. Failures (edit / run / extractor) surface NA rows IES driver handle. New vignette: apsim-callback — synthetic linear-Gaussian recovery demo plus disabled apsimx example. New benchmark script: inst/benchmarks/d4_callback_vs_pst.R. pesto_ies_callback() records obs_target, obs_sd, weights result list manifest emitter full IES context capture.
